files.notes: This file contains a single comprehensive memorial article about President Joseph F. Smith, including biographical information, tributes from his wives and family, a tribute from Bishop Charles W. Nibley, and concludes with his Vision of the Redemption of the Dead. The article flows continuously from beginning to end without clear breaks into separate articles.

files.notes: This file contains two distinct articles. The first is tributes from three leading women about President Joseph F. Smith, and the second is President Smith's vision of the redemption of the dead. The content flows naturally from the memorial theme established in the previous sections.

splits.start_boundary: Heart of the Household By Ruth Moench Bell

files.notes: This file contains a single complete story/article about the Badger family implementing military-style discipline in their household during wartime. The story runs from the title through the end of the file with embedded running headers that should be stripped.

files.notes: This file contains a single article about Indian traditions and customs, written by C. L. Christensen, a pioneer Indian scout. The article includes an introduction about Lamanite cousins and then transitions into 'MOQUI TRADITIONS' which is Part I of what appears to be a multi-part series. The content describes various Indian ceremonies and dances observed by the author during his time with the Moqui villages in Arizona in 1876.

files.notes: This file contains 4 distinct lesson sections. Lesson II appears to be just a header with no content. The lessons cover theology/Book of Mormon prophecy, work/business (header only), genealogy focusing on Scottish and Irish surnames, and home ethics covering national aspects of parenthood.
